steps.title: Graph the four choices
steps.content: A quadratic equation has no real solutions when its graph never touches the x-axis. In Desmos, graph the left side of each answer choice as a function of $x$. The choice whose graph has no x-intercepts is the answer.

steps.title: Read the intercepts
steps.content: Choices A and C cross the x-axis because their constant term is negative, so they have real solutions. Choice B, $y=x^2-14x+49$, equals $(x-7)^2$, so it touches the x-axis once at $x=7$. Choice D opens upward and stays above the x-axis, so it has no real x-intercepts.

steps.title: Confirm with the discriminant
steps.content: For choice D, $a=5$, $b=-14$, and $c=49$. The discriminant is $b^2-4ac=196-4(5)(49)=196-980=-784<0$, confirming no real solutions. Choice D is correct.
